espn.name: Orlando City SC at Inter Miami CF

headlines.description: Martín Ojeda scored a hat trick, Tyrese Spicer had the go-ahead goal in the third minute of stoppage time and Orlando City stunned Inter Miami 4-3 on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ojeda gets hat trick as Orlando stuns Miami

markets.question: Will Orlando City SC win on 2026-05-02?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 2, 2026
If Orlando City SC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.groupItemTitle: Draw (Inter Miami CF vs. Orlando City SC)

eventMetadata.context_description: Orlando City SC's stunning 4-3 comeback victory over Eastern Conference rivals Inter Miami CF on May 2 at Nu Stadium has driven trader consensus to near-certainty on the away win, reflecting the official MLS result. Inter Miami surged to a 3-0 halftime lead via Ian Fray (4'), Telasco Segovia (25'), and Lionel Messi (33'), but Orlando mounted a historic rally—only the third from 3-0 down in league history—with Martín Ojeda's hat-trick (39', 68', 78' penalty) and Tyrese Spicer's 90'+3 winner, despite Miami's 65% possession and 26 shots. Orlando, 14th with a -17 goal difference, capitalized on Miami's third-place vulnerabilities. Reversal scenarios, like a rare MLS protest upholding a different scoreline, remain highly improbable.
